title: Open Question on s((Z/kZ)^n) for fixed k
statement_text: It remains an interesting question to estimate $s((\mathbb{Z}/k\mathbb{Z})^n)$ when $k$ is fixed and $n$ is large.
context_text: Alon and Dubiner proved $s((\mathbb{Z}/k\mathbb{Z})^n) \leq (cn\log n)^n k$ for some absolute constant $c$, showing that for any fixed $n$ the quantity grows linearly in $k$. Elsholtz obtained lower bounds $s((\mathbb{Z}/k\mathbb{Z})^n) \geq 2.08^n$ for odd $k\geq 3$ and large $n$, but a large gap remains between these bounds and the upper bounds of Corollary 2 for fixed odd $k\geq 3$ and large $n$.
notes: Stated as a general open problem in the field with no labeled environment; no specific attribution to prior work given.
paper_title: Erdős-Ginzburg-Ziv constants by avoiding three-term arithmetic progressions
